Flux 2 Multiple Angles LoRA | [image-to-image]
Black Forest Labs' Flux 2 architecture with Multiple Angles LoRA delivers parametric camera control at $0.021 per megapixel. Trading prompt-based perspective interpretation for numeric precision, this model rotates objects through 360° azimuth and 60° elevation using exact degree inputs instead of natural language descriptions. Built for product visualization and 3D asset workflows where geometric accuracy matters more than creative interpretation.
Use Cases:
E-commerce product photography | 3D model reference generation | Multi-angle asset creation
Performance
At $0.021/MP vs $0.039+ for general-purpose image editing models, Flux 2 Lora Gallery delivers 2x cost efficiency for angle-specific transformations.
Metric
Result

Vertical angle control (0°=eye-level, 30°=elevated, 60°=high-angle)

Related Endpoints
Flux 2 Edit
,
Flux 2 LoRA
Standard editing vs LoRA training vs angle manipulation
Parametric Control Over Prompt-Based Rotation
Unlike general image-to-image models that interpret "rotate left" or "view from above" through natural language, Flux 2 Lora Gallery uses three numeric parameters: horizontal_angle (azimuth), vertical_angle (elevation), and zoom (distance). This LoRA-enhanced approach trades creative flexibility for mathematical precision. You specify 45° rotation and get exactly 45°, not an AI's interpretation of "slightly angled."
What this means for you:
Exact angle reproduction:
Generate product shots at 0°, 90°, 180°, 270° for consistent e-commerce galleries without prompt engineering trial-and-error
Elevation control up to 60°:
Produce high-angle shots (looking down) for overhead views or maintain eye-level perspectives with numeric precision
Zoom parameter (0-10 range):
Control camera distance from wide establishing shots (0) to close-up detail views (10) without cropping or resolution loss
Batch processing up to 4 images:
Generate multiple viewing angles in a single API call at $0.021/MP per image

Enterprise Pricing
How It Stacks Up
Flux 2 Edit ($0.039/MP)
– Flux 2 Multiple Angles LoRA ($0.021/MP) specializes in parametric angle control for object rotation, 1.9x more cost-effective. Flux 2 Edit prioritizes natural language editing instructions for broader image manipulation tasks where creative interpretation matters more than geometric precision.
FLUX.1 [dev] Image to Image ($0.025/MP)
– Flux 2 Multiple Angles LoRA ($0.021/MP) delivers dedicated angle manipulation through numeric parameters at 16% lower cost. FLUX.1 [dev] offers general-purpose image-to-image transformation with prompt-based control for workflows requiring creative flexibility over mathematical precision.
FLUX.1 Kontext [pro] ($0.055/MP)
– Flux 2 Multiple Angles LoRA ($0.021/MP) focuses on single-object rotation at 2.6x lower cost. Kontext [pro] handles multi-image reference composition with up to 4 input images for complex scene assembly where contextual understanding outweighs angle-specific control.
